summary: Cavallo's July 2025 monthly newsletter covering mini therapy ponies in Cavallo CLB boots at Vancouver Airport and Trek boots spotted on Beyonce's horse during the Beyonce Bowl.
Complete user manual for the Cavallo LEB Long Ear Boot, a donkey-specific hoof boot, covering measuring, sizing, fitting, break-in, care, and troubleshooting.
Cavallo 'What's New?' hub linking to the newsletter, media features (Beyonce, Oscars, Queen Elizabeth II's pony) and the video library for hoof care content.
Article on using Cavallo Hoof Boots for therapy, rehab and turnout, covering conditions like laminitis, navicular, abscesses and white line disease, and how the shock-absorbing soles aid healing.
Cavallo's August 2025 newsletter featuring the new LEB donkey boot on The Humble Hoof podcast and a request for vet-recommended customer stories.
Cavallo's October 2025 ('Hoof-o-ween') newsletter on supporting laminitis recovery with Cavallo boots and a feature on The Humble Hoof podiatry clinic.
Cavallo's August 2024 newsletter with a Liberty Festival ticket contest tease and a feature on the documentary 'The Cowboy and the Queen' about Monty Roberts and Queen Elizabeth II.
Cavallo's September 2024 newsletter announcing the upcoming Consteed AI 'smart' diagnostic hoof boot and featuring the Texas Tech Masked Rider's horse wearing Trek boots.
Product page for the Cavallo LEB 'Long Ear Boot' for donkeys in turquoise, sold in pairs, donkey-specific sizing D00-D5; used for trail packing, transport, driving, rehab and turnout.
Product page for the Cavallo Trek/BFB Colour Pop Straps Pack, 3 pairs of exchangeable hoof boot straps in hunter orange, bright yellow and turquoise for added security and easy boot identification.
Cavallo's February 2026 newsletter featuring Stampede of Love mini therapy horses in Cavallo CLB boots meeting Buddhist monks on a peace walk, and a recap of the AAEP equine practitioners meeting.
Winter hoof care guide for horse owners covering trim scheduling, breakover, moisture management, and preventing thrush and white line disease in cold, muddy conditions.
